reference: Hi and welcome to Health Care Management. Albumin is often detected in urine, and this can be a sign of kidney glomerular damage or urinary infection. Blood is a sign of infection or kidney stones. You should do ultrasounds to evaluate if there are stones or kidney damage. Also, you should check creatinine and urea levels. You shouldn't be worried; this can be easily treated with antibiotics and certain medications. I wish you good health.
text: Hi and welcome to healthcare management. Albumin is often detected in urine, and this can be a sign of kidney glomerular damage or urinary infection. Blood is a sign of important kidney stones. You should do ultrasounds to evaluate if there are stones or kidney damage. Also, you should check creatinine and urea levels. You shouldn't be worried, this can be easily treated with antibiotics and medications. I wish you good health.
